title: rootfs: Add support for rootfs-overlay.
body:
Allows users to specify a readonly rootfs with `:O`, in exchange podman will create a writable overlay.

Add support for `podman run --rootfs /some/readonly/path:O echo hello`.
Depends Upon: https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/3434

Idea is to re-use `graphRoot` ( i.e  `<base>`+ `containers/storage/`+ `overlay-containers` ) to sustain overlay-ed `rootfs`.

Closes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11038

Yes, just update go.mod with the buildah vendor ID, and we can get this merged.  We only need new version when we are preparing a release.

As best I can tell, your new `buildah-tests.diff` looks good; and the `bud` tests are passing, which is further confirmation. Does that address your concern? If not, could you remind me of your question?

I don't think it made it, and we don't cherry-pick new features as a general policy, so it will unfortunately have to wait for the 4.0 release.

I am trying to use this feature in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/11956 but I am getting the following reproducible error:

```
podman (overlay-rootfs) $ ./bin/podman run --rm --rootfs /usr/libexec/podman/pause:O /pause                                                                                                                                         
^CShutting down, got signal: Interrupt                                                                                                                                                                                              
ERRO[0002] Removing container 515096b0b17c46b444a1a92dfb15db4474bbfe2efa663a13ca6176d8752e3fd0: error removing container 515096b0b17c46b444a1a92dfb15db4474bbfe2efa663a13ca6176d8752e3fd0 root filesystem: 2 errors occurred:       
        * unlinkat /home/vrothberg/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/515096b0b17c46b444a1a92dfb15db4474bbfe2efa663a13ca6176d8752e3fd0/rootfs: directory not empty                                                  
        * unlinkat /home/vrothberg/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/515096b0b17c46b444a1a92dfb15db4474bbfe2efa663a13ca6176d8752e3fd0/rootfs: directory not empty                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
```

I noticed that this PR merged without tests, which is something we should absolutely avoid, especially for new features.
Note, I am currently investigating the error.
@vrothberg Ackd @giuseppe reported this few days back. Cleanups are leaking i'll push a fix with https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/11937 with tests

@flouthoc, the following diff fixes the issue.  Note that I'd prefer not having to reconstruct the path when unmounting.  I think it would be cleaner to store the path to the overlay mount.  Maybe we can replace the `bool` in the config with a `string` to store that information?

```diff
diff --git a/libpod/container_internal.go b/libpod/container_internal.go              
index 3f9738411e80..2678ad496da9 100644                                               
--- a/libpod/container_internal.go                                                    
+++ b/libpod/container_internal.go                                                    
@@ -1692,7 +1692,7 @@ func (c *Container) cleanupStorage() error {                    
                                                                                      
        // umount rootfs overlay if it was created                                    
        if c.config.RootfsOverlay {                                                   
-               overlayBasePath := c.runtime.store.GraphRoot()                        
+               overlayBasePath := filepath.Dir(c.config.StaticDir)                   
                overlayBasePath = filepath.Join(overlayBasePath, "rootfs")            
                if err := overlay.Unmount(overlayBasePath); err != nil {              
                        // If the container can't remove content report the error     
```
@vrothberg I can confirm diff fixes the leaking cleanup. Thanks a lot. :smile:
